preview_text:
The model favors Iran at 65% to win, with New Zealand given a 10% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap; Iran is the clear favorite under the statistical forecast, though the underdog's probability is not negligible.

The 25% draw probability suggests the match has meaningful potential for a stalemate. The spread between Iran's win probability and the alternatives widens toward the favorite, indicating the model expects Iran to control the contest more often than not. A New Zealand upset remains within the tails of the distribution rather than a close-call scenario.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on Iran's chances. Sportsbooks place Iran at 52%, roughly 13 percentage points below the model's 65% estimate. Market consensus and Polymarket are closely aligned with each other at the underdog end, both near 20% for New Zealand. The dashboard does not claim to resolve which assessment is correct; the divergence warrants attention from those building positions.
